free_text: Revised cross sections of the S-32(d,p)S-33 reactions
Cranfield University,RMCS Shrivenham,Swindon.
The HVEC AN2500 Van de Graaff accelerator
Two different experimental methods were used
one  using a thin Cd-S film and the other using a thick
Zn-S target.
The beam current was kept between 2 and 5
nanoAmperes.
No information about uncertainties. Error bars are
given only on the figures with text: The errors is the
standard deviation in the counting  statistics of the
appropriate sulphur reaction.
The resulting cross sections are compared to
previously published data and the S-32(d,p(0))S-33 and
S-32(d,p(1))S-33 cross sections are also compared
directly to the Rutherford cross section.
Last checking has been done.
SD: Major corrections in all Subents.
Subent 002 was split in two. Subent 004 added.
The energies at which the thick film cross section
utility produced data points were not consistent
across the range of incident beam energies.
To calculate a mean cross section, data points were
established at energy increments of 5 keV by linear
interpolation
The thick zinc sulphide sample
The thick film method was employed at different
incident beam energies and the results were combined
into a single cross section. The values agree with
thin film results from the same set of experiments,
which generally exceed those of Saad et al (1966) by
a factor of 2. See REL -REF.
The scattering of deuterons from zinc was assumed to
be purely Rutherfordian as the mass of zinc is
relatively high and the deuteron energies were
relatively low. The nuclear reaction cross sections
for sulphur could thus be directly compared to the
Rutherford cross section for zinc in the same
spectrum when the differing stopping powers are taken
into account.
The 500 mum active thickness of the detector
was used.
- By compiler - Values for excitations level were
derived by compiler from Q-values given at IBANDL.
From the author through DB IBANDL:
https://www-nds.iaea.org/exfor/ibandl.htm
SD: SF9=RECOM -> DERIV in REACTION code.
PART-DET, EN-SEC deleted. ANALYSIS added. Part of data
moved to Subent 003. E-EXC -> Q-VAL (as it is given in
the IBANDL).
The energies at which the thick film cross section
utility produced data points were not consistent
across the range of incident beam energies.
To calculate a mean cross section, data points were
established at energy increments of 5 keV by linear
interpolation
The thick zinc sulphide sample
The thick film method was employed at different
incident beam energies and the results were combined
into a single cross section. The values agree with
thin film results from the same set of experiments,
which generally exceed those of Saad et al (1966) by
a factor of 2. See REL -REF.
The scattering of deuterons from zinc was assumed to
be purely Rutherfordian as the mass of zinc is
relatively high and the deuteron energies were
relatively low. The nuclear reaction cross sections
for sulphur could thus be directly compared to the
Rutherford cross section for zinc in the same
spectrum when the differing stopping powers are taken
into account.
The 500 mum active thickness of the detector
was used.
- By compiler - Values for excitations level were
derived by compiler from Q-values given at IBANDL.

SD Part of data from Subent 002.
A thin film of Cadmium Sulphide
The foil thickness was 40 mu-m for spectra
taken with deuteron energies exceeding 1.65 MeV and
a foil thickness of 30 !lm for 1.65 MeV and under.
The 150 mm2 circular detector
Statistical error from graphic bars
Figs 1,3 from Nucl.Instr.Meth.,B136-138(1998)66
